stem:
The table below shows selected values of two linear functions $f$ and $g$.

| $x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ |
|-----|--------|--------|
| $1$ | $4$ | $14$ |
| $4$ | $13$ | $8$ |

For what value of $x$ does $f(x) = g(x)$?

distractorRationale.A: Reverses the $y$-value order when computing $f$'s slope: uses $\frac{4-13}{4-1} = -3$ instead of $\frac{13-4}{4-1} = 3$, giving $f(x) = -3x+7$; then solves $-3x+7=-2x+16 \Rightarrow -x=9 \Rightarrow x=-9$.
distractorRationale.B: Reads $g(4) = 8$ as $g$'s $y$-intercept instead of computing it from the slope: uses $g(x) = -2x+8$; then solves $3x+1=-2x+8 \Rightarrow 5x=7 \Rightarrow x=\frac{7}{5}$.
distractorRationale.C: Correct: $f(x)=3x+1$ and $g(x)=-2x+16$; setting equal gives $5x=15 \Rightarrow x=3$.
distractorRationale.D: Reverses the $y$-value order when computing $g$'s slope: uses $\frac{14-8}{4-1} = 2$ instead of $\frac{8-14}{4-1} = -2$, giving $g(x) = 2x+12$; then solves $3x+1=2x+12 \Rightarrow x=11$.
